You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/card_template.html`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/BackendImporting.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/PostRequestHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`
- `build.gradle`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

37â†’        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38â†’        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {

53â†’        private val path: String,
54â†’        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55â†’    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56â†’        /**
57â†’         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58â†’         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59â†’         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60â†’         */
61â†’        private var isDone = false

64â†’            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65â†’            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66â†’            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67â†’                super.onPageFinished(view, url)

69â†’        }

74â†’                url == null -> false
75â†’                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76â†’                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77â†’                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78â†’                    false
79â†’                }
80â†’                else -> false

    outPath: String,
    withScheduling: Boolean,
    withMedia: Boolean,
    limit: ExportLimit,
    legacy: Boolean = true
) {
    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
        this.withMedia = withMedia
        this.legacy = legacy
        this.withDeckConfigs = true
    }
    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
}
